annotations.label: Graph minor

anchor.snippet: is called a minor of the graph

annotations.note: Graph minors are not formalized (explicitly flagged as future work in Combinatorics/Graph/Basic.lean); only the analogous matroid minor relation exists.

annotations.label: Wagner's theorem (planarity)

anchor.snippet: a graph is planar if and only if its minors include neither

annotations.note: Planar graphs are not defined in Mathlib (listed as a TODO), so this characterization is absent.

annotations.label: Robertson–Seymour forbidden minor characterization

anchor.snippet: an analogous forbidden minor characterization exists for every property

annotations.note: No graph minor theory exists in Mathlib, so the Robertson–Seymour theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Polynomial-time minor testing

anchor.snippet: it is possible to test whether

annotations.note: This is a computational-complexity result with no graph-minor or complexity formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Edge contraction

anchor.snippet: An edge contraction is an operation that removes an edge

annotations.note: Graph edge contraction is not defined; the analogous matroid contraction operation `Matroid.contract` (／) exists instead.

annotations.label: Minor (formal)

anchor.snippet: is a minor of another undirected graph

annotations.note: Only the matroid minor relation (contraction then deletion) is formalized, not the graph version.

annotations.label: Order independence of contractions and deletions

anchor.snippet: The order in which a sequence of such contractions and deletions is performed

annotations.note: Order-independence of contractions/deletions is proven for matroids (`contract_delete_comm`, `contract_comm`, `contract_delete_contract_delete`), but not for graphs.

annotations.label: Rank under deletion and contraction

anchor.snippet: edge deletions leave the rank of a graph unchanged

annotations.note: Matroid rank behaviour under contraction/deletion is developed, but graph rank under minor operations is not formalized.

annotations.label: Minor-monotone function

anchor.snippet: is referred to as

annotations.note: No notion of a minor-monotone graph parameter exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: H is a minor of G

anchor.snippet: graph H is a minor of graph G

annotations.note: A worked graph-minor example has no formalized counterpart since graph minors are absent.

annotations.label: Minor relation is a partial order

anchor.snippet: the graph minor relation forms a partial order

annotations.note: A `PartialOrder` instance for the minor order exists for matroids (refl/trans/antisymm lemmas), but not for graphs.

annotations.label: Robertson–Seymour well-quasi-ordering (Wagner's conjecture)

anchor.snippet: this partial order is actually a well-quasi-ordering

annotations.note: The general WQO concept is defined, but the theorem that the graph minor order is a well-quasi-ordering is not formalized.

annotations.label: Graph structure theorem

anchor.snippet: also prove the graph structure theorem

annotations.note: The Robertson–Seymour graph structure theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: H-minor-free graphs are sparse

anchor.snippet: the simple H -minor-free graphs must be sparse

annotations.note: No results on edge bounds for H-minor-free graphs exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Separator theorem for H-minor-free graphs

anchor.snippet: have a separator theorem similar to the planar separator theorem

annotations.note: Neither graph separators nor the planar separator theorem are formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Treewidth bound for H-minor-free graphs

anchor.snippet: H -minor-free graphs have treewidth

annotations.note: Treewidth is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hadwiger conjecture

anchor.snippet: The Hadwiger conjecture in graph theory proposes that

annotations.note: The Hadwiger conjecture relating chromatic number and clique minors is not formalized.

annotations.label: Case k=5 is the four color theorem

anchor.snippet: is a restatement of the four color theorem

annotations.note: The four color theorem is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Snark theorem

anchor.snippet: the snark theorem announced by

annotations.note: Snarks and the snark theorem are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Minor-closed family

anchor.snippet: such a class is said to be minor-closed

annotations.note: There is no notion of a minor-closed family of graphs in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Planar and surface-embeddable graphs are minor-closed

anchor.snippet: form minor-closed families

annotations.note: Planar/surface-embeddable graphs and minor-closure are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finite forbidden minor characterization of minor-closed families

anchor.snippet: can be characterized as the family of

annotations.note: The finite forbidden-minor characterization (Robertson–Seymour) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Wagner's theorem characterizing planar graphs

anchor.snippet: characterizing the planar graphs as the graphs having neither

annotations.note: Planarity and the K5/K3,3 forbidden-minor characterization are not formalized.

annotations.label: Bounded pathwidth iff forbidden minor is a forest

anchor.snippet: has bounded pathwidth if and only if its forbidden minors include a forest

annotations.note: Pathwidth is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bounded tree-depth iff forbidden disjoint union of paths

anchor.snippet: has bounded tree-depth if and only if its forbidden minors include

annotations.note: Tree-depth is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bounded treewidth iff forbidden minor is planar

anchor.snippet: has bounded treewidth if and only if its forbidden minors include a planar graph

annotations.note: Treewidth and planar graphs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bounded local treewidth iff forbidden apex graph

anchor.snippet: has bounded local treewidth

annotations.note: Local treewidth and apex graphs are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Simplified structure theorem for crossing-number-one H

anchor.snippet: then the H -minor-free graphs have a simplified structure theorem

annotations.note: No graph structure theorems or crossing numbers are formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wagner's characterization of K5-free graphs

anchor.snippet: the K 5 -free graphs are exactly the 3-clique-sums of planar graphs

annotations.note: Clique-sums and planar graphs are not defined, so this characterization is absent.

annotations.label: Topological minor

anchor.snippet: is called a topological minor of a graph

annotations.note: Topological minors (subdivisions) are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Topological minor is a minor

anchor.snippet: Every topological minor is also a minor

annotations.note: Neither topological minors nor graph minors are formalized, so this implication is absent.

annotations.label: Converse holds for maximum degree at most three

anchor.snippet: but holds for graph with maximum degree not greater than three

annotations.note: This degree-≤3 equivalence of minors and topological minors is not formalized.

annotations.label: Topological minor relation is not a well-quasi-ordering

anchor.snippet: The topological minor relation is not a well-quasi-ordering

annotations.note: Topological minors are not defined, so this non-WQO result is absent.

annotations.label: Induced minor

anchor.snippet: is called an induced minor of a graph

annotations.note: Induced minors are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Induced minor of a string graph is a string graph

anchor.snippet: every induced minor of a string graph is another string graph

annotations.note: String graphs and induced minors are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: String graphs have infinitely many induced-minor obstacles

anchor.snippet: with infinitely many obstacles in the induced minor order

annotations.note: String graphs and the induced-minor order are not formalized.

annotations.label: Lifting operation

anchor.snippet: is the operation that deletes the two edges

annotations.note: The edge-lifting operation is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Immersion minor (via lifting)

anchor.snippet: we say that H is an immersion minor of G

annotations.note: Graph immersions are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Immersion minor (edge-disjoint paths)

anchor.snippet: if there exists an injective mapping from vertices

annotations.note: The edge-disjoint-paths definition of immersion is not formalized.

annotations.label: Immersion minor relation is a well-quasi-ordering

anchor.snippet: The immersion minor relation is a well-quasi-ordering

annotations.note: The generic WQO notion exists, but graph immersions and this theorem are not formalized.

annotations.label: Immersion minors as planarizations

anchor.snippet: immersion minors arise as the planarizations of non-planar graphs

annotations.note: Immersions and planarizations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shallow minor

anchor.snippet: is a minor in which the edges of

annotations.note: Shallow minors are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Minor via vertex-disjoint subtrees

anchor.snippet: the vertices of H can be represented by a collection of vertex-disjoint subtrees

annotations.note: The subtree-model definition of a graph minor is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Odd minor

anchor.snippet: An odd minor restricts this definition by adding parity conditions

annotations.note: Odd minors are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Forbidden odd minors need not be sparse

anchor.snippet: graphs with forbidden odd minors are not necessarily sparse

annotations.note: Odd minors and density results about them are not formalized.

annotations.label: Bipartite minor

anchor.snippet: is a bipartite minor of another graph

annotations.note: Bipartite minors are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wagner's theorem for bipartite minors

anchor.snippet: A bipartite graph G is a planar graph if and only if it does not have the utility graph

annotations.note: Planarity and bipartite-minor characterizations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Minor containment is NP-complete

anchor.snippet: is NP-complete in general

annotations.note: NP-completeness of minor containment is a complexity result not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fixed H solvable in polynomial time

anchor.snippet: it can be solved in polynomial time

annotations.note: This algorithmic complexity result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polynomial-time recognition of minor-closed families

anchor.snippet: to recognize the members of any minor-closed family in polynomial time

annotations.note: No minor-closed families or complexity results are formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linear-time test for fixed planar H

anchor.snippet: we can test in linear time in an input graph

annotations.note: This linear-time algorithmic result for planar H is not formalized in Mathlib.
